place: Milltown borough, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Milltown’s historic Raritan River Railroad Station, once a bustling hub for the Raritan Railroad, now serves as a preserved landmark for the borough.
The Milltown Historical Society, founded by dedicated volunteers, collects and shares stories that trace the borough’s rich past and immigrant experiences.
In 1917, a local newspaper dubbed Milltown “the greatest little town in all the land,” a nickname still celebrated by residents today.
Milltown’s Main Street hosts a self‑guided walking tour that guides visitors through historic buildings and the town’s original 19th‑century architecture.
